what_it_does: This file is a ledger/model card for the deployed BAAI/bge-m3 embedding model, recording its architecture, serving configuration, benchmarked throughput, co-residency behavior, known quirks, open design questions, and links to related models and runtimes.
observations: Status is deployed-default with runtime vllm on port 8001
Model has three output heads: dense (1024-d), sparse lexical, and ColBERT multi-vector (~30 x 128-d)
The vLLM endpoint exposes only the dense head; sparse and ColBERT require FlagEmbedding
Throughput saturates around concurrency 32 at ~1,370 embeddings/s
Co-resident test with qwen3_5-4b-awq succeeded at 86-97% GPU util
Semantic separation on the cat/feline/stocks probe is weaker than jina-embeddings-v5-text-small
Native context is 8,192 tokens; longer inputs need chunking
standouts: A single model provides dense, sparse, and ColBERT outputs, but the deployed vLLM path only serves the dense head
Throughput plateaus at concurrency 32 while latency keeps rising
Successful co-residency with a chat model under simultaneous load
Pure dense similarity is noticeably weaker than a comparison embedder on the same probe
conventions: YAML frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, port, dates, and tags
Wikilink cross-references to related models and runtimes
Performance table with concurrency, throughput, and latency
Known-good launch config in a fenced code block
Sections for source, fits-these-needs, quirks, open questions, and connects-to
concerns: Sparse/ColBERT production serving path is unresolved
The deployed endpoint does not expose two of the model's three advertised output heads
Semantic separation on the probe is weaker than an alternative, suggesting pure dense may be insufficient
8K context cap means quality degrades silently on longer inputs
No head-to-head evaluation on actual corpora
notes: A deployed-default embedding model card that documents how to serve BGE-M3 dense via vLLM, while flagging that its sparse and ColBERT heads still need a production path.
questions: Which sparse/ColBERT path will be chosen: FlagEmbedding sidecar or index-time multi-vector storage?
What chunk size will be used for Obsidian markdown, Xero text, and code?
Does hybrid BGE-M3 retrieval outperform dense plus a domain-specific embedder for the actual corpora?
Why is GPU memory utilization capped at 0.30 and is that still optimal?
What does deployed-default imply about fallback or comparison models?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document a deployed foundation model, and the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Operational model serving and resource budgeting
Co-residency of embedding and chat services
Hybrid retrieval over pure dense embedding
intention_signals: The file is trying to capture enough operational knowledge about BGE-M3 that the team can reproduce the serve setup, understand its real-world limits, and decide how to expose sparse/ColBERT outputs in production.
novelty: It documents a three-head embedding model where the primary deployed runtime only serves one head, and it includes a co-resident load test with a chat model.
contribution: It contributes the concrete deployment configuration, measured throughput/latency, co-residency evidence, and known limitations for BGE-M3 that a fused system would retain as the operational reference for this model.
summary_for_embedding: A model card for the deployed BAAI/bge-m3 dense/sparse/ColBERT embedding model served via vLLM on port 8001, including launch config, throughput benchmarks, co-residency results, and unresolved sparse/ColBERT serving path.
intention_for_embedding: To record how BGE-M3 is currently operated, what it can and cannot serve, and what design decisions remain open for hybrid retrieval.
